plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) to eliminate risk.
Sell semiconductors (SMH) to reduce sector concentration.
Trim income (QQQI) and broad index (SPY, QQQ) holdings.
Buy ANET, a top-rated setup, for quality growth.
This plan prioritizes capital preservation with a cash buffer.
conservative.read: This plan targets roughly 1.5% per month by focusing on capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, accepting lower potential upside for a much lower expected drawdown.

plan: Trim leveraged ETFs (SOXL) and exit others (SPAL).
Reduce semiconductor (SMH) and income (QQQI) exposure.
Trim broad index (SPY) to reduce concentration.
Add ANET and NVDU, strong quality setups, for growth.
This plan balances risk reduction with growth opportunities.
balanced.read: This plan aims for about 2.5% per month by de-risking while adding quality growth names. It reduces leverage and concentration, accepting moderate potential upside and drawdown.

plan: Increase leveraged ETF (SOXL) exposure for aggressive growth.
Exit income (QQQI) and trim broad index (SPY, QQQ) holdings.
Add top-rated ANET and ERX for concentrated growth.
This plan leverages high-conviction setups and ETFs.
Expect higher potential gains and significantly larger drawdowns.
aggressive.read: This plan targets aggressive growth of about 4% per month by leaning into leverage and high-scoring setups. It accepts substantially higher potential upside and significantly larger drawdowns due to leverage and daily rebalancing of ETFs.

plan: Sell all leveraged ETFs and high-beta single names.
Sell semiconductors (SMH) to reduce volatility.
Prioritize income ETF (QQQI) and maintain broad index (SPY, QQQ).
This plan focuses on steady monthly distributions and capital preservation.
Expect low growth and minimal drawdown, prioritizing yield.
income.read: This plan targets approximately 1% per month by prioritizing income generation and capital preservation. It minimizes volatility and leverage, accepting very low growth in exchange for stable monthly distributions and reduced drawdown risk.
